title: Two-Level System Defects: The Microscopic Noise Source
description: Explore the physics of two-level system (TLS) defects that plague solid-state quantum devices. Learn about their microscopic origins, spectral signatures, and the ongoing battle to eliminate them through materials engineering.
learningObjectives: Describe the standard tunneling model for two-level systems
Identify TLS signatures in qubit spectroscopy and coherence measurements
Calculate TLS-induced frequency shifts and decoherence rates
Analyze TLS saturation effects and their implications for qubit operation
Evaluate materials strategies for TLS reduction
